description: §6.9 hoisting negative — the SAME shape as pinned-forward-ref but WITHOUT `pinned`: a plain `<count> = 0` hoists to file scope (§6.9.2), so the earlier reference resolves and E-STATE-PINNED-FORWARD-REF does NOT fire. This is the pinned-vs-hoisted contrast.
rationale: §6.9.2: within a structural scope ALL state-cell declarations are collected before any reactive read/render, so reads can refer to declarations that appear later. Removing `pinned` re-enables hoisting → no forward-ref error.
